name: Convex Quadratic Programming
applicable_scenarios: minimize a positive-semidefinite quadratic objective under linear constraints
SVM dual, least-squares with bound constraints
math_form: \min_x \tfrac{1}{2} x^T Q x + c^T x \text{ s.t. } G x \le h, \; A x = b, \; Q \succeq 0
python_template:
import numpy as np
import cvxpy as cp

def solve_qp(Q, c, G=None, h=None, A=None, b=None):
    n = Q.shape[0]
    x = cp.Variable(n)
    constraints = []
    if G is not None:
        constraints.append(G @ x <= h)
    if A is not None:
        constraints.append(A @ x == b)
    obj = cp.Minimize(0.5 * cp.quad_form(x, cp.psd_wrap(Q)) + c @ x)
    prob = cp.Problem(obj, constraints)
    prob.solve()
    return x.value, prob.value
common_pitfalls: Q must be PSD; an indefinite Q makes the problem non-convex
large dense Q is memory-heavy; exploit structure (sparse / low-rank)
active-set methods scale poorly beyond ~10k variables; prefer OSQP
